commit.message:
fix(scripts): post-increment exits script under set -e

bash `((pass++))` with pass=0 evaluates to 0, which returns
exit code 1 — causing immediate exit under `set -euo pipefail`.
Switch to pre-increment `((++pass))` which always evaluates to
a non-zero value.

Fixes script stopping after first check on Raspberry Pi.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui): phased restart progress with BT/MA status

Replace simple counter with multi-phase restart indicator:
- Phase 1: Saving configuration
- Phase 2: Stopping service
- Phase 3: Waiting for service to start (with elapsed timer)
- Phase 4: Monitoring BT and MA connectivity
- Phase 5: Final status summary with BT/MA indicators

Add ma_connected field to /api/status response for restart
progress tracking. Timeout extended to 40s for service start,
plus 30s for BT/MA connection monitoring.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(mute): sync PulseAudio sink when muting via MA

When muting through MA API (VOLUME_VIA_MA), the PA sink was not
being muted/unmuted — only the MA player state was updated.
This caused audio to keep playing even after muting via UI.
Now set_sink_mute() is called alongside the MA command.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(mute): prevent SSE from reverting optimistic mute state

SSE status updates were overwriting the optimistic mute toggle
before MA's echo arrived, causing the icon to flicker back.
Add a 2-second debounce window: after a user mute click, SSE
updates preserve the user's desired mute state instead of
replacing it with stale server data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(mute): optimistic UI update and reduced timeouts

- Mute icon toggles immediately on click (optimistic update),
  reverts on failure — eliminates perceived multi-second delay
- Reduce MA command timeout: 5s→2s in _set_mute_via_ma(),
  10s→3s in execute_cmd() — mute is a fast operation
- Pass explicit mute value to API to avoid toggle race conditions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(auth): add Music Assistant as web UI auth provider

Add MA credentials as a third authentication backend alongside HA
login_flow and local password. When AUTH_ENABLED is True and the bridge
is connected to MA, users can sign in with their MA username/password.

Backend:
- Add _detect_auth_methods() to auto-detect available methods (ma/ha/password)
- Add _ma_validate_credentials() using existing _ma_http_login() from api_ma
- Route login POST by method field instead of ha_mode boolean
- Pass auth_methods list to login template for dynamic UI

Frontend (login.html):
- Tab-based method switcher when multiple methods available
- Separate forms for MA, HA, and local password
- Empty state message when no auth method is configured

Config UI:
- Auth hint shows available sign-in methods when enabled
- Password labeled as optional when MA is connected

Tests:
- 10 new tests for method detection and credential validation
- All 148 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
UX: polish tenant "Prijavi kvar" form + audit category/priority whitelist alignment (#469)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct tenant issue payload to match SU_sanitizeIssue storage contract

- Rename buildingId -> bid (critical: was causing SU_sanitizeIssue to return null)
- Rename createdBy -> createdByUser, add createdByRole: ROLE_STANAR
- Change priority default/fallback from "normal" to "medium" (not in valid list)
- Fix HTML select options: replace invalid "normal" option with low/medium/high/urgent

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: show tenant-submitted issue marker in issues list and detail

- List row: adds "Prijavio stanar [· username]" in .issMeta when createdByRole === "stanar"
- Detail panel: adds "Prijavio: [username|stanar]" meta item with --stanar highlight
- styles.css: two minimal rules (.issMetaRole--stanar, .issDetailMetaItem--stanar) for blue highlight
- Graceful: badge omitted when createdByRole is missing; username omitted when createdByUser is missing

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: align kvar form categories with SU_sanitizeIssue whitelist

The prijaviKvarCat <select> had 'instalacije' and 'elektrika' options which
are not in SU_sanitizeIssue's VALID_CATEGORIES. Selecting them caused the
category to be silently remapped to "ostalo" on every save/reload.

Replaced form options with the full sanitizer whitelist:
ostalo (default/selected), lift, struja, voda, interfon, vrata, ciscenje

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* ux: polish Prijavi kvar form labels, placeholders and option text

- Title label: "Naslov" → "Kratki naslov"
- Desc label: "Opis" → "Opis kvara"
- Desc placeholder: guides tenant to describe location, symptom and duration
- Category options: raw keys → readable Serbian (values unchanged)

commit.message:
fix(ui): reload config form after MA login to preserve new token

After successful MA login, the backend saves the new token directly to
config.json. But the config form still had the OLD token in its hidden
field, so 'Save and Restart' would overwrite the new token with the old.

Now calls loadConfig() after login to refresh the form with the
backend-saved values before marking dirty.

commit.message:
fix(auth): always try direct HTTP fallback on library login failure

The music_assistant_client library raises 'Invalid username or password'
for any 401 response, including format mismatches with MA beta. The
previous code short-circuited the direct HTTP fallback when it saw
'invalid' in the error message, preventing login from ever succeeding
with MA beta 2.8+.

Now always falls through to _ma_http_login() which correctly tries
both body formats and reports the actual error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
fix(auth): support MA beta 2.8+ login format

MA beta 2.8.0b19 changed /auth/login to expect credentials in a
nested dict: {"credentials": {"username": ..., "password": ...}}
instead of flat {"username": ..., "password": ...}.

Add _ma_http_login() fallback that tries both formats when the
music_assistant_client library fails. Also update _get_ma_oauth_params()
to try both stable GET and beta JSONRPC endpoints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Redirect unscoped WP URLs back to scoped WP URLs in service worker (#3370)

## Summary
- When the service worker intercepts a request from a scoped page
(identified via the Referer header) but the request URL itself is
unscoped, redirect it to the scoped version of the URL instead of
directly handling it as a PHP request. This prevents scope loss when
navigating around a scoped WordPress instance.
- Skip intercepting cross-origin requests initiated from scoped pages,
letting them pass through to the network instead.

## Context

Previously, when a scoped WordPress page linked to an unscoped URL (e.g.
a WordPress-generated link without the scope prefix), the service worker
would directly handle it via `handleScopedRequest()`. This worked for
that single request, but the resulting page would be loaded at an
unscoped URL. Navigating from that unscoped page to another unscoped URL
resulted in losing the scope entirely, and the service worker would no
longer be able to match requests to the correct WordPress instance.

By redirecting to the scoped URL instead, the browser's address bar and
subsequent navigations all stay within the scope.

## Test plan
- [ ] Load a scoped WordPress Playground instance
- [ ] Navigate to a WordPress link that doesn't include the scope prefix
- [ ] Verify the browser redirects to the scoped version of the URL
- [ ] Verify subsequent navigation continues to work within the scope
- [ ] Verify cross-origin requests from scoped pages are not intercepted

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
optimize: avoid repeated align param calculation in DirectIOObject.Set (#1638)

This change optimizes the DirectIOObject class by moving the computation
of align_bit, align_size, and align_mask from the Set() method to the
constructors. Previously, these values were recalculated every time Set()
was called, which is unnecessary since they only depend on a configuration
value that doesn't change during the object's lifetime.

Changes:
- Added header include for vsag/options.h
- Modified default constructor to initialize alignment parameters
- Modified parameterized constructor to delegate to default constructor
- Removed redundant alignment parameter calculations from Set() method

Fixes #1636

Signed-off-by: LHT129 <tianlan.lht@antgroup.com>
Co-authored-by: Kimi-2.5 <assistant@opencode.ai>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
refactor: improve error handling, types, and documentation

Preserve original error context in ScheduleFetchError, tighten formatter
map types with enum unions for compile-time exhaustiveness, and document
why LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams requires a transaction client.

Changes:
- Add cause option to ScheduleFetchError constructor and pass original error
- Add JSDoc to LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams explaining FOR UPDATE requirement
- Type SKILL_LEVEL_LABELS and LEARNING_STYLE_LABELS with enum Record types
- Narrow formatSkillLevel/formatLearningStyle parameters to enum unions
- Remove formatter tests for invalid string inputs (now compile errors)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): improve rate limit headers and error metadata

getRateLimitHeaders included Retry-After on every response, violating
RFC 6585 which reserves it for 429/503. RateLimitError only carried
retryAfter but the class supports limit/remaining/reset fields.

Changes:
- Remove Retry-After from getRateLimitHeaders (X-RateLimit-* only)
- Include limit, remaining, and reset in RateLimitError on rejection
- Update tests to reflect Retry-After removal from success headers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hooks): centralize stream cleanup in useRetryGeneration

Early returns on terminal SSE events (complete, error, cancelled) did
not cancel the reader or abort the controller, causing resource leaks.
Wrapping the reader loop in try/finally ensures cleanup on every exit
path.

Changes:
- Wrap reader loop and buffer-flush in inner try/finally block
- Call reader.cancel() and controller.abort() in finally clause

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): bind OAuth state token redemption to provider

The state redemption was not bound to the issuing provider, allowing a
token issued for one provider to be redeemed against another. consume()
now requires and verifies a provider parameter, preventing cross-provider
token replay attacks.

Changes:
- Add provider parameter to OAuthStateStore.consume interface
- Add eq(provider) to the WHERE clause in consume implementation
- Update validateOAuthStateToken to require and forward provider
- Pass google_calendar provider in Google OAuth callback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(logging): align correlation ID header with proxy

The proxy sets x-correlation-id on every request, but REQUEST_ID_HEADER
in request-context.ts was x-request-id. createRequestContext therefore
never found the correlation ID the proxy set, generating a fresh UUID
and breaking request correlation across the stack.

Changes:
- Change REQUEST_ID_HEADER from x-request-id to x-correlation-id
- attachRequestIdHeader automatically picks up the new value

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: apply code quality improvements across helpers and tests

Batch of small improvements from code review: improve JSDoc, add return
types, export interfaces for testability, make non-transactional DB
helpers more ergonomic, and add test isolation.

Changes:
- Expand readHeader JSDoc with param/return docs and HeaderSource behavior
- Add explicit SQL | undefined return type to userAttemptsSincePredicate
- Make dbClient optional in OwnedPlanQueryParams (defaults to getDb())
- Add LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams with required dbClient for lockOwnedPlanById
- Export OAuthStateStore interface for typed test doubles
- Add beforeEach resetDbForIntegrationTestFile in monitoring-queries test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: validate weekNumber in getWeekBoundaries

Add upfront validation that weekNumber is an integer >= 1, throwing a
RangeError for zero, negative, or non-integer values that would produce
shifted or partial week boundaries.

Changes:
- Add Number.isInteger and >= 1 check with RangeError
- Add test cases for weekNumber 0, -1, and 1.5

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent double-firing and flush decoder in useRetryGeneration

Add a ref-based synchronous guard to prevent concurrent retry POST
requests from rapid clicks. Flush the TextDecoder after the read loop
to process any remaining buffered bytes before falling through to the
error state.

Changes:
- Add retryInFlightRef to block concurrent in-flight requests
- Reset ref in finally block, independent of cooldown timer
- Flush decoder with stream:false after read loop exits
- Process remaining buffer lines for terminal SSE events

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): wrap schedule generation errors in ScheduleFetchError

Wrap distributeTasksToSessions in try-catch to maintain a consistent API
error contract. Add SCHEDULE_GENERATION_FAILED error code for non-weekly-
hours failures instead of misleading INVALID_WEEKLY_HOURS.

Changes:
- Add SCHEDULE_GENERATION_FAILED to SCHEDULE_FETCH_ERROR_CODE
- Catch errors from distributeTasksToSessions and rethrow as ScheduleFetchError
- Update actions test mock to include new error code

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: harden formatters against edge cases

Fix formatMinutes to treat only null/undefined/NaN/Infinity/negative as
missing (0 now correctly returns '0 min'). Fix formatWeeklyHours to round
to 2 decimal places avoiding floating-point noise. Replace ad-hoc switch
statements in formatSkillLevel and formatLearningStyle with Record lookups.

Changes:
- Guard negative minutes with minutes < 0 check
- Round hours via parseFloat(hours.toFixed(2)) before formatting
- Replace switch/case with SKILL_LEVEL_LABELS and LEARNING_STYLE_LABELS maps
- Update tests for new 0/negative/NaN/float behaviors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): harden IP validation and implement true sliding window

Replace permissive IPv6 regex patterns with Node's net.isIP() for strict
IP validation, and convert the fixed-window rate limiter to a true
sliding window using per-request timestamps.

Changes:
- Replace IPV4_PATTERN, IPV6_PATTERN, IPV6_V4_PATTERN with net.isIP()
- Change WindowEntry from {count, windowStart} to {timestamps: number[]}
- Prune expired timestamps on each check, only record allowed requests
- Compute retryAfter from oldest active timestamp
- Update getRemainingRequests and getResetTime for timestamp-based logic

commit.message:
[CK TILE ENGINE] Add grouped_gemm operator to Tile Engine (gfx942/gfx950) (#4996)

## Motivation

The grouped_gemm CK Tile kernel exists (e.g.,
`example/17_grouped_gemm/`) but has no Tile Engine wrapper. Grouped GEMM
handles multiple independent GEMM problems with varying M/N/K dimensions
in a single kernel launch. This PR adds the Tile Engine infrastructure
for automated kernel generation, benchmarking, and profiling of grouped
GEMM kernels.

Jira: AICK-809

## Technical Details

- Created Tile Engine wrapper under `tile_engine/ops/gemm/grouped_gemm/`
following the `gemm_universal` template
- Files added: `CMakeLists.txt`, `grouped_gemm_common.hpp`,
`grouped_gemm_benchmark.hpp`, `grouped_gemm_profiler.hpp`,
`grouped_gemm_benchmark.py`, `grouped_gemm_benchmark_single.cpp`,
`grouped_gemm_instance_builder.py`, `configs/`
- Supported datatypes: fp16, fp8, bf16, bf8
- Supported layouts: rcr, rrr, ccr, crr
- Target GPUs: gfx942, gfx950
- CK Tile kernel: `ck_tile::GroupedGemmKernel` from
`include/ck_tile/ops/gemm/kernel/grouped_gemm_kernel.hpp`
- Instance builder extends `GemmKernelBuilder` base class
- Registered in `tile_engine/ops/gemm/CMakeLists.txt`
- Updated Jenkinsfile to build and benchmark grouped_gemm targets in CI
- Benchmark infrastructure includes JSON output, CSV export, and
verification support

## Test Plan

- CMake configure succeeds for grouped_gemm targets
- Kernel instance builder generates valid kernel headers for all
(datatype, layout) combinations
- At least one kernel binary compiles and runs per datatype/layout
combination
- Correctness passes with `--verify 1` on gfx942/gfx950

## Test Result



## Submission Checklist

- [ ] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: refactor OmnibusProvider to use omnibus-ts library (#17)

* feat: refactor OmnibusProvider to use @waterloorocketry/omnibus-ts library

Replaces raw socket.io-client usage with the communicator() API from
omnibus-ts. This provides typed message handling with Zod validation,
automatic snake_case to camelCase conversion, and channel-based
message routing.

Changes:
- OmnibusProvider uses communicator() instead of io()
- Removed manual OmnibusPayload/OmnibusMessage types (now from library)
- Updated mock backend to use msgpack parser and channel-based events
- Removed debug console.log statements
- Connection status still uses allowExposeSocket until library supports
  events natively

Closes waterloo-rocketry/2025-software-issues#87

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use omnibus-ts connection API instead of allowExposeSocket

Replace raw socket event listeners with the new
connection.onConnectionChange() API from omnibus-ts. This removes
the need for allowExposeSocket: true just to monitor connection status.

- Remove allowExposeSocket flag from communicator options
- Replace socket.on('connect'/'disconnect'/'connect_error') with
  comm.connection.onConnectionChange()
- Import ConnectionStatus from @waterloorocketry/omnibus-ts instead
  of local types
- Remove local ConnectionStatus type (now provided by omnibus-ts)

Depends on: waterloo-rocketry/omnibus-ts#6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump @waterloorocketry/omnibus-ts from 0.2.0 to 0.3.0

Bump omnibus-ts to v0.3.0 which includes the connection lifecycle API
(onConnectionChange, isConnected) already used by OmnibusProvider.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: average sensor samples and use message-level timestamp

Replace per-sample last-value logic with averaging all values in the
array, guard against empty arrays, and use the message-level timestamp
(baseTimestamp * 1000) instead of computing per-sample timestamps from
relativeTimestamps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add missing workflow files from OSPO actions

Added standard workflows used across OSPO GitHub Actions:

- auto-labeler.yml: Automatically labels PRs based on release-drafter config
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: Documents Copilot CLI setup for contributors
- linter.yaml: Runs super-linter on PRs to enforce code quality
- pr-title.yml: Validates PR titles follow conventional commit format
- scorecard.yml: Runs OpenSSF Scorecard security analysis
- stale.yaml: Closes stale issues after 35 days of inactivity
- release-drafter.yml: Configuration for auto-labeler and release notes

These workflows provide:
- Automated PR labeling for release notes
- Code quality enforcement via linting
- Security scanning via Scorecard
- Stale issue management
- PR title validation

Brings pr-conflict-detector in line with other OSPO actions like
issue-metrics, stale-repos, contributors, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: increase weather damage to make game meaningful

- BASE_DAMAGE=5 ensures every tick causes damage regardless of weather
- Rain multiplier 10x (was 2x), wind divisor 3 (was 5)
- Block HP reduced: dry_sand=25, wet_sand=40, packed_sand=60 (was 40/60/100)
- MAX_HEALTH=60, REINFORCE_AMOUNT=15
- Weather fetch now uses max(rain, precipitation) to catch drizzle
- dry_sand survives ~5 calm ticks, packed_sand ~12 — meaningful decay

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
